Location update interval in seconds (0 =: Location update interval in seconds (0 = disabled)
Paste the complete callback URL from you: Paste the complete callback URL from your browser after Tesla authentication
Update interval in seconds (0 = disabled: Update interval in seconds (0 = disabled, minimum 10)
Update interval while driving in seconds: Update interval while driving in seconds (0 = disabled)
Wake up vehicle for each update (prevent: Wake up vehicle for each update (prevents sleep to save battery)
cost_awake_hours_help: Hours per day the vehicle is not asleep (charging + driving + other activity). The adapter polls continuously while charging.
cost_estimate_hint: Estimated cost for regular vehicle_data polling per vehicle. Values update automatically.
cost_free_budget_help: Tesla currently grants a 10 EUR free credit per account per month.
cost_price_per_1000_help: Tesla Fleet API currently charges about 2 EUR per 1,000 vehicle_data calls.
excludeElementList_examples: Example: charge_state, climate_state, drive_state, vehicle_state, vehicle_config, location_data, charge_history, live_status, backup_history, energy_history, self_consumption_history, self_consumption_history_lifetime, energy_history_lifetime
reset_login_help: Use this after changing scopes in the Tesla Developer App or when the login should be recreated. Save once with reset enabled; the adapter deletes the stored Fleet session and Code URL and restarts. Then generate a new Auth Link, authorize Tesla again and paste the new callback URL.
step1_instruction1: Click "Generate Key Pair" below to create an EC key pair (prime256v1)
step1_instruction2: Copy the Public Key and go to fleetkey.net - paste it under "Host Public Key" and create a subdomain (e.g. abc123.fleetkey.net)
step1_instruction3: After saving, FleetKey.net hosts your Public Key. Tesla will download the key from there during registration.
step2_instruction1: Create a Fleet API Application at
step2_instruction2: Origin: https://<your-subdomain>.fleetkey.net (your full FleetKey subdomain, e.g. https://abc123.fleetkey.net)
step2_instruction4: Copy Client ID and Client Secret from the created app and enter them below together with your FleetKey domain
step3_instruction1: Click "Generate Auth Link" - a new browser tab opens with the Tesla login page
step3_instruction2: Log in with your Tesla account and authorize the app
step3_instruction3: After login you will see "Page Not Found" - this is expected! Copy the complete URL from the browser address bar
step3_instruction4: Paste the URL into the Code URL field below and click Save and close
step3_warning: Never share this URL with anyone! It grants access to your Tesla account.
step4_description: The Virtual Key is required to send commands to your vehicle (lock/unlock, climate, charging, etc.). Without it, you can only read vehicle data. You can do this step after the adapter is running.
step4_instruction1: Open the URL below on your phone in the Tesla App, or scan the QR code with your phone camera
step4_instruction2: The Tesla App will ask you to confirm adding a "third-party key"
step4_instruction3: Go to your vehicle and hold your key card to the center console to confirm the installation
step4_qr_hint: Scan this QR code with your phone to open the URL directly in the Tesla App
telemetry_bridge_description: Configure a local vehicle-command proxy plus an MQTT-fed Fleet Telemetry server. The adapter updates live Tesla states from MQTT and can periodically synchronize Fleet API data not covered by telemetry.
telemetry_config_preview_note: This preview is saved into telemetryFieldsJson and sent to the vehicle-command proxy when configuring Fleet Telemetry.
telemetry_fallback_poll_hint: Uses the normal update interval to periodically refresh Fleet API data. Set the update interval to 0 to disable scheduled API synchronization.
telemetry_fields_category_help: Enable the fields you want to send in this category. Empty minimum-delta values use the displayed adapter default where available.
telemetry_fields_description: Select exactly which Fleet Telemetry fields should be configured on the vehicle, define the minimum update interval per field and optionally set a minimum delta. Tesla still sends values only when the value changed and the configured interval has elapsed.
telemetry_fields_grouped_help: The Tesla field catalogue is split into collapsible category groups. Expand only the category you want to edit, so the page remains responsive even with all Fleet Telemetry fields.
telemetry_fields_json_expert_help: Legacy/expert field. Only needed when no field table is saved yet or for future Tesla fields not included in the table.
telemetry_fields_json_help: Leave empty to use the adapter default preset. Alternatively enter a JSON object such as {"Soc":{"interval_seconds":60,"minimum_delta":1}} or a {"fields":{...}} wrapper.
telemetry_fields_note: Fields tagged with default are the adapter preset. Minimum change suppresses small numeric changes; when the field is left empty and a placeholder is shown, the adapter uses that default value (for example 100 m for location). Location fields require the vehicle_location OAuth scope. Unmapped fields are stored under <VIN>.telemetry.fields.<FieldName> as raw telemetry values.
telemetry_fields_tab_hint: The Fleet Telemetry field selection is configured on the Fleet Telemetry fields tab. The adapter stores that selection internally as JSON for compatibility with older versions.
telemetry_fields_table_help: Enable the fields Tesla should send via Fleet Telemetry. Interval is the minimum time between two emitted changes. Minimum change may stay empty; then the adapter uses the displayed default value, for example 1 % for state of charge or 100 m for location. Tesla still sends data only when the value changed and the interval has elapsed. Switch between the category tabs and use the table filters to search or show enabled fields.
telemetry_fields_table_help_short: Enabled rows are included in the Fleet Telemetry configuration. Empty minimum change uses the displayed default value.
telemetry_invalid_json_default: Telemetry fields JSON is invalid. Using default preset.
telemetry_minimum_delta_default_hint: Leave empty to use the default value {value}.
telemetry_server_ca: Telemetry server CA / full chain PEM
telemetry_server_ca_help: Paste the CA or full certificate chain in PEM format that should be embedded into the Tesla vehicle configuration.
